column9: C/METAL E2E: "the best way to make a cup of tea?" 300ms/tok. GPU attn scores REVERTED (too slow for short ctx)
C/METAL peak: 189ms/tok — DISCARDED: measured with warm Metal LRU cache (9.8GB) that was later deleted. Not reproducible with trust-OS approach.
C/METAL: BLAS delta-net (0.28ms vs 0.78ms cpu_attn), GCD dispatch, gated timing. 352ms/tok avg.
mmap expert files: CATASTROPHIC 5x regression. Per-page faults 100x slower than pread for uncached 7MB experts.
Malloc zero-copy cache 2581 entries (18GB): 52% hit but GPU memory pressure + page cache loss = slower
Malloc zero-copy cache 500 entries (3.5GB): 37% hit, still slower than Metal LRU (2.93 vs 3.14)
Speculative early routing: 53% prediction accuracy but cache pollution + overhead = 38% slower
F_RDADVISE kernel hints: no improvement, added syscall overhead
GPU combine+norm in CMD3: deferred_wait eliminated (0.83->0ms) but absorbed into cmd1_wait (0.49->1.78ms). Cleaner pipeline.
GPU RoPE + fused CMD1+attention for full-attn layers. 4.76ms/layer (was 5.53). Quality verified.
STABLE: GPU combine+norm + BLAS delta-net + fused full-attn CMD + 500 LRU cache. Cold cache 2.75-2.85 tok/s.
4-bit warm cache: 4.20 tok/s — DISCARDED: measured with Metal LRU cache. Not reproducible with trust-OS.
4-bit WARM: 4.80 avg, 5.31 peak — DISCARDED: measured with Metal LRU cache (later deleted). Not reproducible.
2-BIT EXPERTS: 5.55 tok/s sustained 80 tokens! Quality: 'Classic Coin Analogy...light switch...Heads or'. EXCELLENT.
2-BIT 200tok: 5.61 tok/s sustained. Quality: math(255=15*17 CORRECT), code(sorted+key=len), science(plant analogy)
F_NOCACHE + 2-bit: 5.68 avg, 5.91 peak. Bypass page cache avoids thrashing 120GB data.
Default fused GPU delta-net: 3.90ms/layer vs 4.15ms with --cpu-linear fallback on the same tea prompt. Output identical ("to use a teapot.").
Removing the CMD2->CPU->CMD3 bounce regressed: 4.07ms/layer vs 3.70 baseline and 3.99 tok/s vs 4.40. Output still matched the tea prompt.
GPU routing softmax+topK removed routing_cpu but increased cmd2_wait: 3.96ms/layer vs 3.84 baseline and 4.13 tok/s vs 4.24.
Persistent expert pread pool beats dispatch_apply: tea A/B averaged 3.81ms/layer vs 3.97 baseline and 4.28 tok/s vs 4.09. Output identical ("to use a teapot.").
Fused GPU final-norm + lm_head tail was mixed and slightly slower overall: 4.01ms/layer vs 3.93 baseline and 4.04 tok/s vs 4.13.

Indexed expert cache makes a larger hotset viable: 2500-entry Metal cache averaged 3.75ms/layer and 4.35 tok/s vs 4.09ms/layer and 3.98 tok/s at 1500 entries on the fixed ocean prompt. 3500+ entries regressed despite higher hit rate, so 2500 is the new sweet spot.
2-bit + trust OS (no cache): 5.74 tok/s. Deleted Metal LRU cache entirely. OS page cache manages better.
Peak single-token 2-bit: 7.05 tok/s (warm cache). Quality excellent for math/code/reasoning.
Back to 4-bit for tool calling quality. 2-bit broke JSON output (quotes → backslashes).
4-bit trust OS baseline: 3.90 tok/s cold steady-state. K=4, no cache, page cache only.
FMA dequant kernel: rearrange to fma(nibble, scale*x, bias*x). GPU compute -5 to -11%.
LZ4 compressed experts (209→175GB): -13% from decompress overhead on warm cache reads.
F_RDADVISE prefetch during GPU compute: expert_io -31% but cmd2_wait +73% from unified memory DMA contention. Net 0%.
Spin-poll GPU wait: CPU thermal competes with GPU on unified architecture. -23%.
GPU LUT dequant kernel (v5): indirect register access serializes on Apple GPU. -2%.
GPU private buffer compression: blit shared→private before matvec. Isolated -13.5% but 4×7MB blit cost exceeds savings in pipeline.
dispatch_io: -70% slower than pread (dispatch_data overhead kills it).
Expert file clustering: NVMe doesn't care about scatter distance at 7MB read granularity. 0%.
